description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

Beadsmith is a Tauri + React desktop client for Beadwork. Beadwork remains the durable source of truth; Beadsmith should read structured `bw` JSON and present it without creating a second issue tracker. The current shipped slice renders the three-pane Issue Explorer chrome and the Issue List from `bw list --all --json`, but the data model and RPC/API names still describe that data as `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries`.

The parent epic establishes a more precise model: an **Issue** is the canonical Beadwork data loaded from the Current Workspace, while **Issue List** and **Issue Detail** are view-specific presentations of that same Issue. `bw list --all --json` already contains the fields needed for Issue Detail, including description and comments, so this epic should not add a new `bw show` call or detail-specific RPC.

This bead is the enabling prefactor. It keeps the user-visible Issue List behavior intact while renaming and expanding the contract so later beads can build Issue Detail without fighting summary-oriented names or inventing a second model.

## What to build

Rename the existing Issue List contract from `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` to `Issue` / `list_issues` across the Rust adapter/RPC boundary, generated TypeScript bindings, frontend Effect service, compact list view model, tests, and relevant documentation touched by this slice.

Expand the normalized Issue contract returned by the existing `bw list --all --json` path so every Issue includes the fields needed by future detail rendering:

- `description`: always-present string; missing/null raw descriptions become `""`.
- `comments`: always-present array; missing/null/omitted raw comments become `[]`.
- comment `author`: always-present string; missing author becomes `""` and is hidden later by view logic.
- `closeReason`: always-present string; missing/null raw close reasons become `""` and is hidden later by view logic.

Keep the current loading behavior and failure model exactly recognizable: loading, success with Issues, empty with workspace path, and typed failure.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Beadwork is the source of truth. Do not introduce local durable Issue state or a parallel Issue Detail model.
- Follow ADR-0003: consume structured `bw` JSON only; do not parse TTY/Markdown CLI output.
- Follow ADR-0004: Rust IPC goes through TauRPC/Specta, and React calls that through the Effect service pattern. Do not add direct `@tauri-apps/api/core.invoke` calls from React.
- Do not add a separate `bw show` call for this epic. The full Issue record should come from the existing list call.
- Preserve the raw Beadwork date/string values. Formatting is deliberately out of scope for this epic.
- This bead should be as behavior-preserving as possible except for contract shape and names.

## Testing guidance

Preserve the current adapter/RPC/Effect seams through the rename. Extend tests at those seams to prove the new fields are normalized from `bw list --all --json` and exposed through the generated/frontend contract. Keep compact Issue List view-model tests because the list still exists as a scan-oriented presentation over Issue data.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Existing `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` naming is replaced with `Issue` / `list_issues` across code, generated bindings, tests, and relevant docs touched by this slice.
- [ ] `bw list --all --json` remains the only Beadwork read used for the Issue List and future Issue Detail data.
- [ ] The normalized Issue contract includes always-present `description`, `comments`, comment `author`, and `closeReason` fields with empty defaults when Beadwork omits or nulls them.
- [ ] The raw Beadwork `defer_until` field continues to surface to the frontend as `deferUntil`.
- [ ] Existing list-loading states still behave as before: loading, success with Issues, empty with workspace path, and typed failure.
- [ ] Adapter tests prove the `bw list --all --json` invocation and normalization for description, comments, author, and closeReason.
- [ ] RPC/binding tests prove the expanded Issue contract is exported to TypeScript.
- [ ] Effect service tests are renamed/refactored to Issue/listIssues terminology and still cover success, empty, and failure behavior.
- [ ] Compact Issue List view-model tests are preserved under Issue terminology.
- [ ] No Issue selection, Markdown rendering, comments UI, dependency UI, or E2E expansion is implemented in this bead.

## Out of scope

- No `bw show` integration.
- No Issue Detail rendering.
- No Markdown renderer.
- No comment presentation.
- No dependency/metadata presentation beyond carrying existing fields in the Issue contract.
- No route, URL, history, refresh, or workspace-switch behavior.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
title: Rename Issue List contract to Issue
